title: Design Notes
text: file records design decisions made working milestones blueprint.md. entry says decided, , decision lives code. decision changes, update first.

title: Design Notes
text: fmls object data frame subclass two structural attributes one behavioral attribute: formula matrix (data frame ): one row per formula, one column per term, cells 1/0 marking membership. patterns produce every later layer reads. termTable: vec_proxy() tm vector — one row per term role, side, label, group tier, transformation, levels, attributes. Terms side meta (strata, random effects) live , never matrix, instruct fit rather fit. instructions: list behavioral instructions ride along family; currently subsets, named list quosures captured subset_data(). Reconstruction rules: formulas_to_terms() reassembles row tm vector matrix membership, always re-attaching strata random terms; formula.tm() renders fitting formula, dropping meta terms except random effects, render (1 | term).

title: Design Notes
text: Random effects role (random, shortcut .r()), side meta. y ~ .x(x) + .r(id) lme4-native y ~ x + (1 | id) parse term. Random slopes (wt | id) carried whole re-rendered parentheses. Data subsets role. filter like sex == "F" variable formula; instruction data. Subsets therefore live fmls instructions attribute via subset_data() expand fitting plan, exactly like strata levels . main design fork M1 reason .f() rune.

what: Transformations keep the call as the name (M1)
title: Design Notes
text: log(x) stays log(x) — term’s name full call, formulas rebuild losslessly without mesa needing re-apply anything. wrapper additionally recorded transformation field downstream layers (e.g. set_data(), future table labeling) can interpret . factor()/ordered() wrappers make set_data() classify term converted variable.

what: The parser is an AST walk (M1)
title: Design Notes
text: tm.formula() walks formula’s syntax tree (collect_formula_terms()) instead scanning .names() positionally. Consequences worth remembering: Nested runes now work: .x(log(x)) exposure log transformation. Unrecognized calls (Surv(...), cluster(...)) opaque terms, carried whole. Group tiers accept number digits (.g10). .()/.m() shortcuts demote (warning) exposure exists; explicit :b products keep interaction role silently.

title: Design Notes
text: register_pattern(name, fn) adds tm -> tbl_df function environment-backed registry; fmls() apply_pattern() look patterns name, formula_patterns() lists registered. rolling_interaction stub removed rather finished — returns can done properly, registered pattern.

what: Combining families keeps the first definition (M2, issue #42)
title: Design Notes
text: c() / vec_c() fmls merges term tables; term arrives conflicting definitions, left-wins message names conflicted terms. message, warning, keep-first intended resolution — message resolution never silent.

title: Design Notes
text: fit_plan(object, data) crosses formulas x strata levels x subsets inspectable tibble; fit() executes row row. Decisions inside: .fn resolves name match.call() (function, string, parsnip model_spec); old positional match.call()[[3]] bug regression-tested. parsnip spec, fit() runs parsnip::fit() unwraps engine fit, mdl() table layers see familiar objects. .models whitelist retired; remains documentation. Failures soft: error becomes recorded condition (raw = TRUE) mdl stub whose summaryInfo$error carries message; model_table() turns fit_status = FALSE, flatten_models() drops unfit rows. Fitted calls normalized (function name, formula text, data name) models compare cleanly hand-fit references don’t embed copies data. Subset provenance recorded dataArgs$subsetName surfaces subset column mdl_tbl.

what: Degrees of freedom and the variance-covariance matrix (M4)
title: Design Notes
text: degrees_freedom now comes stats::df.residual() nobs - length(coef) fallback (old nrow - ncol - 1 lm-shaped guess, one even lm). full var_cov matrix stays summaryInfo: estimate_interaction() needs covariance exposure product term compute interaction confidence intervals without original data.

title: Design Notes
text: ggplot2 scales stay Imports: forest-plot column core deliverable table layer. survival moved Suggests: touched hazard tables (pyears()), now guard requireNamespace(). Dispatching coxph objects require package. parsnip, lme4, broom.mixed Suggests: guarded point use. lifecycle added Imports (badges already use without ).

title: Design Notes
text: Added: .r() random effects; multi-digit .g tiers; nested rune/transformation combinations. Declined now: .f() subset rune (see subsets decision ) per-term weights (clear fitting-layer story yet — revisit M5).

title: Design Notes
text: author-checks AFEQT/CARRS/MIMS targets stores moved tests/manual/ (build-ignored, run testthat) R CMD check clean. Milestone 6 replaces public-data equivalents.

title: The {mesa} Blueprint
text: document guiding map development mesa. exists package grew organically — class class, problem problem — deserves deliberate architecture matches original intent. milestone broken incremental subtasks can picked , completed, checked one time. document source truth direction; GitHub issues triaged , revised thinking evolves.

title: The {mesa} Blueprint
text: mesa modeling epidemiology causality assessment user-friendly way. built epistemological perspective: small building blocks build complex concepts. starts individual model terms, expands join merge formulas, can fit combined together, tested different datasets different modeling approaches. destination grammar presenting multiple models tables papers, built top gt — package called mesa (table upon models laid ). feeling package give fluidity — able pick terms dataset “play” modeling: swap strata, add random effects, subset data, roll interaction , watch family models unfold. causal perspective makes different general-purpose modeling tools: variables roles (exposure, outcome, confounder, mediator, interaction, strata), roles carry meaning shapes formulas expand, models fit, results displayed. building something already exists. ecosystem already solves problem — tidymodels parsnip universal model definitions, broom tidying, gt table rendering — mesa lean rather reinvent .

title: The {mesa} Blueprint
text: package organized layers, built one . epistemology package, file structure, documentation, vignettes follow .

title: The {mesa} Blueprint
text: honest accounting, July 2026 review. test suite passes (10 skips), core pipeline works lm/glm/coxph cases, layered concept sound. implementation drifted intent several places. Revision note (July 2026): Milestones 0 4 complete. Every defect listed fixed regression test, structural debts term, formula, fitting layers paid (gt layer debts remain Milestone 6). Design decisions made along way recorded DESIGN.md. R CMD check runs clean (0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 notes). works well: role vocabulary formula shorthand (.o(), .x(), .c(), .m(), .(), .s(), .g()) — soul package idea right. pipeline fmls() |> fit() |> model_table() |> flatten_models() runs end--end. vctrs-based class scaffolding gives principled vector behavior. Stratified fitting (.s()) already produces one model per stratum level, exactly “play” feeling build . Known defects (found review; one fixed Milestones 0–4): apply_parallel_pattern() subsets grouped covariates group == 0L instead group == g, tier-0 groups ever collected. Fixed M2 two-tier regression test. fit.fmls() recovers .fn positionally via match.call()[[3]], breaks arguments supplied different order. Fixed M4; .fn resolves name accepts function, string, parsnip spec. apply_rolling_interaction_pattern() unfinished stub returns outcome/exposure grid without rolling anything. Removed M2; returns registered pattern can done properly. mdl_tbl_cast() drops dataList attribute combining tables — root issue #26 (model-table.R). Still open — Milestone 5. lifecycle::badge() used throughout documentation lifecycle declared DESCRIPTION. Fixed M0. Group tiers parse single digit (.g1 .g9), .transformations supports log. Fixed M1; multi-digit tiers fuller transformation vocabulary. degrees_freedom computed nrow - ncol - 1 every model family, correct outside lm. Fixed M4 via df.residual() per-family fallback. (Found M1) documented ~ c("Manual", "Automatic") labeling idiom deparsed junk string labeled_formulas_to_named_list(). Fixed; vector values now evaluate properly across table functions. Structural debts: tm.formula() parser walks .names() positionally rather formula AST, making fragile hard extend (also ~370 lines one function). Paid M1: recursive AST walk named helpers, round-trip property tests. supported models (.models), patterns (.patterns), roles (.roles), transformations (.transformations) frozen sysdata.rda — closed vocabularies extensible ones wanted. Paid M1/M2: vocabularies live R/vocabulary.R accessors, patterns open registry. gt layer (tbl_beta, tbl_dichotomous_hazard, tbl_categorical_hazard, tbl_interaction_forest) four monoliths duplicated filtering/formatting logic, meaningful tests skipped depend private datasets (AFEQT, CARRS). Still open — Milestone 6. private-data tests moved tests/manual/ automated suite R CMD check stand . Naming split abbreviated spelled-forms (mdl_tbl vs model_table, fmls, tm) without stated convention. Still open — decided Milestone 5. Repository hygiene: sandbox.R ex.R sit top level, built vignette artifacts (getting_started.html, .R) committed, test-dev.R placeholder, test-gt.R empty. Cleaned M0. Random effects — explicitly part vision — representation yet. Built M1–M4: .r() role, (1 | id) parsing rendering, fitting lme4.

title: The {mesa} Blueprint
text: rules check every change . Roles causal, roles first-class. term’s role knowable, updatable, visibly change behavior downstream (expansion, fitting, display). layer speaks neighbors. Terms know nothing models; tables consume model collections, raw fits. Fluidity ceremony. common path — terms dataset, snapped formulas, fit, tabled — read single pipe bookkeeping. Leverage, don’t reinvent. parsnip defines models, broom tidies , gt renders tables. mesa contributes causal grammar connects . Everything round-trips. formula -> tm -> fmls -> formula lossless; mdl_tbl able reconstruct context model holds. Fail softly batches. fitting twenty models, one failure mark fit_status = FALSE, sink fleet.

title: The {mesa} Blueprint
text: milestone scoped subtasks roughly PR-sized. Work top bottom within milestone; milestones 1–2 land 3–4, 0 can interleaved anywhere. Every subtask touches behavior carry tests.

title: The {mesa} Blueprint
text: Housekeeping real work unobstructed. Move sandbox.R ex.R package root (ex.R became inst/examples/interaction-forest.R; sandbox.R referenced private data function longer exists, deleted). Remove committed build artifacts: vignettes/getting_started.html, vignettes/getting_started.R. Add lifecycle DESCRIPTION (badges already use). Delete placeholder test-dev.R empty test-gt.R. Review PR #72 (testthat 3.3.0 compatibility) — fix already exists main, PR superseded can closed GitHub. Audit Imports: ggplot2 scales stay (forest layer core); survival moved Suggests behind requireNamespace() guards. Reasoning recorded DESIGN.md. Organize _pkgdown.yml reference index grammar layer (validated pkgdown::check_pkgdown()). Triage open GitHub issues blueprint (map ). Labeling/closing GitHub remains -hand step. Issue triage map:

title: The {mesa} Blueprint
text: term carry causal identity reliably; parser trustworthy enough build everything else . Write role taxonomy specification: Roles table R/terms.R now gives role causal definition downstream behavior changes (issue #6). Decide representation random effects data subsets: random effects became .r() role (side meta, rendering (1 | term), lme4-native syntax parsed ); subsets deliberately role — fmls instructions via subset_data(). Decision recorded DESIGN.md. Rewrite tm.formula() parser recursive walk formula AST (collect_formula_terms() plus named helpers demotion, interaction expansion, defaults, overrides). Nested runes like .x(log(x)) now work. Fix group tier parsing accept multi-digit tiers (.g10) clean interaction/grouping bookkeeping. Open transformation vocabulary (log family, sqrt, scale, factor, ordered, polynomial/spline markers), move vocabularies sysdata.rda R/vocabulary.R term_roles() / term_transformations() accessors. Add level field categorical terms (issue #5); filled set_data() update(). Simplify format.tm() role → color lookup (.role_colors R/vocabulary.R). Property-style round-trip tests test-roundtrip.R: plain formulas, role shortcuts, opaque calls, meta terms, random effects.

title: The {mesa} Blueprint
text: Formulas terms joined rules; patterns rules, small open grammar rather closed switch statement. Document internal representation (formula matrix + term table + instructions) DESIGN.md. Fix parallel-pattern grouping bug (group == 0L → group == g) regression test using two grouped tiers. Turn patterns registry: register_pattern() adds tm -> tibble function name, formula_patterns() lists , fmls() looks — test registering user-defined pattern. Remove apply_rolling_interaction_pattern() stub; returns registered pattern can done properly. Define formula combination semantics (issue #42): c() vec_c() merge families; conflicting term definitions resolve left--wins explicit message. Document mediation triad (total effect, mediator model, direct effect) fmls() Mediation section. Un-skip repair fmls/formula interchange test — directions plus casting now round-trip. Address formula shorthand requests (issue #25): .r() multi-digit tiers added; .f() subset rune declined (see DESIGN.md).

what: Milestone 3 — Fluency: playing with terms against data ✅
title: The {mesa} Blueprint
text: milestone makes package feel way supposed feel: pick terms dataset play. Grow data-classification helpers “meet data” step: set_data(x, data) (methods tm fmls) stamps type, distribution, levels onto terms, classifying transformed terms underlying variable. Design implement fluent verb layer fmls (R/fluency.R): add_strata(), remove_strata(), add_terms(), remove_terms(), swap_outcome(), subset_data() — pipeable, returning modified family. Extending mdl_tbl waits Milestone 5. Make strata data-aware: set_data(), print method shows strata: (2 levels) anything fit. Implement data subsets first-class instructions: subset_data(f, == 1, cyl > 4) records quosures family; [fit()] fits per subset, subset lands provenance model table. Implement random-effects role formula layer: y ~ .x(x) + .r(id) composes, prints, rebuilds y ~ x + (1 | id). Give fmls print worthy play: deck header summarizes formula count, outcomes, exposures, mediators, interactions, strata (levels), random effects, subsets.

what: Milestone 4 — Fitting through a universal interface ✅
title: The {mesa} Blueprint
text: Stop maintaining whitelist model functions; let parsnip define model , keep plain-function escape hatch. Fix positional .fn capture fit.fmls(): .fn now resolves name accepts function, string, parsnip spec, regression test scrambles argument order. Accept parsnip model specifications fit(); .models whitelist retired (survives documentation) engine’s identity flows model_call. Refactor plan--execute: fit_plan(object, data) builds inspectable formula x stratum x subset plan, fit() executes . Fail softly: failed fit becomes recorded error — fit_status = FALSE message model table, warning plus condition object raw mode — flatten_models() skips unfit rows. Support mixed models random-effects role: fit(f, .fn = lme4::lmer, ...) works directly (parsnip mixed-model engine works spec path), tidied broom.mixed. Correct degrees_freedom per model family (df.residual() nobs - coef fallback). Decision var_cov: stays, estimate_interaction() needs exposure-product covariance without original data (see DESIGN.md).

title: The {mesa} Blueprint
text: notebook models: storage, recall, combination must trustworthy table layer can . Note: M4 already delivered part milestone’s groundwork — mdl_tbl gained subset provenance column, fit_status now truthfully reflects failed fits. Fix attribute reconciliation combining tables: mdl_tbl_cast() must carry dataList , combined formula matrices/term tables must deduplicate correctly (issue #26), tests bind tables different datasets. Complete dplyr verb support tests filter, select, mutate, arrange, bind_rows, invariant-column messaging (issue #23). Settle naming convention across package — one canonical public name per class, kept documented alias (e.g. model_table() canonical, mdl_tbl() alias) — state convention design note. Rework flatten_models() exponentiation: infer sensible defaults model family (binomial glm/coxph exponentiate; lm ) rather -name = argument, keeping explicit override. Run validate_model_table() construction, document invariant columns, improve warnings around construction (issues #18, #46). Prune validation.R code referring retired classes (model_archetype) validation reflects real object model.

what: Milestone 6 — The table grammar (the mesa itself)
title: The {mesa} Blueprint
text: destination: grammar laying multiple models publication tables, built gt. Write table grammar specification refactoring code: rows terms levels; columns statistics (beta, confidence interval, n, p); spanners models, adjustment sets, outcomes; row groups strata subgroups. one page governs every tbl_* function (issues #47, #48). Extract shared pipeline four monoliths composable internal steps: select models → flatten → format estimates → lay rows/columns → hand gt; tbl_* function becomes thin recipe steps. Standardize labeled-formula argument handling (labeled_formulas_to_named_list()) single documented mechanism variable-plus-label input across table functions. Replace skipped private-data tests (AFEQT, CARRS) public-data equivalents (mtcars, survival::lung, simulated data) table layer actually covered (currently: test-gt-beta.R, test-gt-forest.R, test-gt-survival.R skips). Stabilize hazard tables (tbl_dichotomous_hazard, tbl_categorical_hazard), including unfinished rate_difference/person_years machinery (issue #30). Stabilize tbl_interaction_forest generalize estimate_interaction() beyond binary interaction terms (categorical levels, per TODO interaction.R). Snapshot tests rendered gt structure table regressions visible review.

title: The {mesa} Blueprint
text: Documentation teaches grammar, clean release. One vignette per layer, order: terms causal roles; formulas patterns; playing data (strata, subsets, random effects); fitting model tables; making mesa (gt layer). Rewrite README around single narrative arc — one dataset, one causal question, terms table — first impression grammar, class list. Update development-log article reflect blueprint current design. vignette article causal reasoning : roles map estimands (total effect, direct effect, effect modification), references (Hill, Pearl, VanderWeele) — intellectual home package. NEWS.md brought current; cran-comments.md refreshed; R CMD check clean platforms; CRAN submission.

title: The {mesa} Blueprint
text: Ideas belong vision block milestones . Parked lost. DAG integration: derive adjustment sets dagitty/ggdag graph hand fmls() pattern — causal diagram becomes formula generator. Effect modification workflows: build outward estimate_interaction() toward fuller effect-measure-modification toolkit (additive vs multiplicative scales, RERI). Model diagnostics attached data: since mdl_tbl can hold datasets, residual assumption checks recalled per model. Sensitivity analyses: E-values unmeasured-confounding checks natural extension role system. Marginal effects: interoperate marginaleffects estimands beyond coefficients.

title: The {mesa} Blueprint
text: Work drawn earliest milestone unchecked boxes; subtask one branch/PR. Every behavioral change lands tests; bugs listed get regression tests referencing document. design decision made (naming, role representation, verb API), record — short “Decisions” note relevant milestone beats lost conversation. direction changes, change document first, code.

title: A Causality-Informed Modeling Approach
text: development version can installed Github. mesa accepted CRAN, can installed CRAN well.
code: # Development version remotes::install_github("shah-in-boots/mesa") # CRAN installation after release install.packages("mesa")

title: A Causality-Informed Modeling Approach
text: package mesa (Models Epidemiology Statistical Analysis) intended way handle causal- epidemiology-based modeling following principles: Role determination variables Generativity formula creation Multiple model management

title: A Causality-Informed Modeling Approach
text: package simple use. mtcars dataset serve example, use linear regressions primary test. toy example shows building six models parallel, key exposure wt term, two outcomes mpg hp.
code: library(mesa) #> Loading required package: vctrs #> Loading required package: tibble #>  #> Attaching package: 'tibble' #> The following object is masked from 'package:vctrs': #>  #>     data_frame  f <- fmls(mpg + hp ~ .x(wt) + disp + cyl + am, pattern = "parallel") m <- fit(f, .fn = lm, data = mtcars, raw = FALSE) mt <- model_table(mileage = m) print(mt) #> <mdl_tbl> #>   id        formula_index data_id name  model_call formula_call outcome exposure #>   <chr>     <list>        <chr>   <chr> <chr>      <chr>        <chr>   <chr>    #> 1 453532fa… <dbl [6]>     mtcars  mile… lm         mpg ~ wt + … mpg     wt       #> 2 d90b656e… <dbl [6]>     mtcars  mile… lm         mpg ~ wt + … mpg     wt       #> 3 5d2f00cc… <dbl [6]>     mtcars  mile… lm         mpg ~ wt + … mpg     wt       #> 4 49495c60… <dbl [6]>     mtcars  mile… lm         hp ~ wt + d… hp      wt       #> 5 dd309c8d… <dbl [6]>     mtcars  mile… lm         hp ~ wt + c… hp      wt       #> 6 b10fc492… <dbl [6]>     mtcars  mile… lm         hp ~ wt + am hp      wt       #> # ℹ 7 more variables: mediator <chr>, interaction <chr>, strata <lgl>, #> #   level <lgl>, model_parameters <list>, model_summary <list>, #> #   fit_status <lgl>

title: A Causality-Informed Modeling Approach
text: several important extended classes package introduces, however primarily used internal validation shortcuts allow effective communication. fmls version base R formula object, contain additional information extra features tm atomic elements used describe individual variables, departs terms generally treated {stats} package mdl mdl_tbl exist primarily tidy versions class regression modeling

See Methods, , details. ... <data-masking> Expressions return logical vector, defined terms variables .data. multiple expressions included, combined & operator. combine expressions using | instead, wrap when_any(). rows expressions evaluate TRUE kept (filter()) dropped (filter_out()).

title: Estimating interaction effect estimates — estimate_interaction
text: using categorical interaction terms mdl_tbl object, estimates interaction terms confidence intervals can evaluated. effect interaction estimates based levels interaction term. estimates intervals can derived estimate_interaction() function. approach based method described Figueiras et al. (1998).

title: Estimating interaction effect estimates — estimate_interaction
code: estimate_interaction(object, exposure, interaction, conf_level = 0.95, ...)

title: Estimating interaction effect estimates — estimate_interaction
text: object mdl_tbl object subset single row exposure exposure variable model interaction interaction variable model conf_level confidence level confidence interval ... Arguments passed methods

title: Estimating interaction effect estimates — estimate_interaction
text: data.frame n = levels(interaction) rows (presence absence interaction term) n = 5 columns: estimate: beta coefficient interaction effect based level conf_low: lower bound confidence interval estimate conf_high: higher bound confidence interval estimate p_value: p-value overall interaction effect across levels nobs: number observations within interaction level level: level interaction term

title: Estimating interaction effect estimates — estimate_interaction
text: estimate_interaction() requires mdl_tbl object single row length. Filtering mdl_tbl occur prior passing function. Additionally, function assumes interaction term binary. categorical, current recommendation use dummy variables corresponding levels prior modeling.

title: Estimating interaction effect estimates — estimate_interaction
text: . Figueiras, J. M. Domenech-Massons, Carmen Cadarso, 'Regression models: calculating confidence intervals effects presence interactions', Statistics Medicine, 17, 2099-2105 (1998)

what: Fit the family of models a fmls object describes — fit.fmls
title: Fit the family of models a fmls object describes — fit.fmls
text: Fitting happens two steps: plan drawn , executed. plan crosses every formula family every stratum level (.s() terms) every subset instruction (subset_data()), one fmls object can quietly describe dozens models. plan can inspected anything fit fit_plan() — "play" step, shape analysis visible runs. Failures soft: one model batch fit, recorded error message (fit_status becomes FALSE downstream model_table()) rather sinking rest fleet.

title: Fit the family of models a fmls object describes — fit.fmls
code: # S3 method for class 'fmls' fit(object, .fn, ..., data, raw = TRUE)

title: Fit the family of models a fmls object describes — fit.fmls
text: object fmls object .fn modeling approach, given : fitting function, e.g. lm lme4::lmer name fitting function, e.g. "glm" {parsnip} model specification, e.g. parsnip::logistic_reg() |> parsnip::set_engine("glm"), lets engine {parsnip} knows serve modeling approach ... Additional arguments passed fitting function (e.g. family = "binomial") data data.frame containing modeling variables raw logical. TRUE (default), returns list fitted model objects fitting function made ({parsnip} specifications, underlying engine fit). FALSE, returns mdl vector carries causal context forward model_table().

title: Fit the family of models a fmls object describes — fit.fmls

what: Fluent verbs for playing with formula families — fluent_verbs
title: Fluent verbs for playing with formula families — fluent_verbs
text: family formulas feel like something play : pick terms dataset, snap together, swap pieces, watch family models unfold. pipeable verbs take fmls object return modified one, modeling deck can grown interactively: add_strata() / remove_strata(): mark terms stratifying variables (fit() fit one model per stratum level) add_terms() / remove_terms(): add drop covariates every formula family swap_outcome(): exchange one outcome another, keeping rest family intact subset_data(): record data-filtering instructions (e.g. sex == "F") fit() apply, fitting family per subset Terms may given bare names strings. swap_outcome(), two-sided formula old ~ new swaps specific outcome; bare name allowed family single outcome.

title: Vectorized formulas — fmls
text: term carries mediator role (.m()), expansion generates causal triad used reason mediation, alongside covariates already requested pattern: outcome ~ exposure + mediator + covariates — exposure effect pathway mediator held open mediator ~ exposure — exposure's effect mediator outcome ~ mediator + exposure — mediator's effect outcome presence exposure Comparing exposure estimate across formulas allows epidemiologist judge much total effect travels mediator (per VanderWeele Robins).

Options c("left", "right", "meta", "unknown"). meta option refers term may apply globally terms (e.g. strata, random effects). label Display-quality label describing variable group Grouping variable name modeling placing terms together. integer value given identify group term . type Type variable, either categorical (qualitative) continuous (quantitative) distribution variable specifically subcategorized, e.g. ordinal, continuous, dichotomous, etc description Option descriptions definitions needed tm, potentially part data dictionary transformation Modification term applied combining data. title: Create vectorized terms — tm
text: Specific roles variable plays within formula. particular importance, serve special terms can effect formula interpreted. role causal definition, role changes behavior downstream — formulas expand (fmls()), models fit (fit()), results displayed. Formulas can condensed applying specific role individual runes function/wrapper. example, y ~ .x(x1) + x2 + x3. signify x1 specific role exposure. Grouped variables slightly different placed together hierarchy tier. indicate group tier, shortcut can integer following .g (multi-digit tiers .g10 allowed). number given, assumed tier (tier zero). Ex: y ~ x1 + .g1(x2) + .g1(x3)

title: Create vectorized terms — tm
text: term wrapped recognized transformation (see term_transformations()) keeps full call name — log(x) remains log(x) formulas rebuild losslessly — additionally records wrapper transformation field downstream interpretation. Unrecognized calls (e.g. survival::Surv(time, status)) carried opaque term names.

title: The mesa vocabulary — formula_patterns
text: grammar {mesa} built small, controlled vocabularies: causal roles term can play, patterns formulas expand, transformations term can carry. accessors expose vocabularies can inspected (error messages can point somewhere authoritative). term_roles() returns named list roles formula shortcuts (e.g. exposure = ".x") term_transformations() returns recognized transformation wrappers (e.g. log()); wrapped term keeps full call name records wrapper transformation field formula_patterns() returns names registered expansion patterns available fmls() (see register_pattern() add one)
